parent.name: Lacey
parent.embed_html:
SpaceX prints Raptor parts only where casting or machining cannot deliver the internal channels and complex geometry.

For everything else, traditional methods win on speed, cost, and scale. The real edge is not adopting every new tool but ruthlessly choosing the process that… pic.twitter.com/aYXGkM3MhQ

— Lacey (@LaceyPresley) May 10, 2026

ai_summary: Lacey Presley explains SpaceX's manufacturing philosophy: 3D printing is used selectively for Raptor engine parts where internal channels and complex geometry cannot be achieved through traditional methods, while casting and machining remain superior for speed, cost, and scale on other components. The insight demonstrates ruthless process optimization—adopting new technology only where it provides genuine advantage rather than for novelty—a critical manufacturing and cost discipline lesson for hardware-focused companies scaling production.
embed_html:

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 10, 2026
